mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending expectations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient corporate output that offsets higher rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 20-day returns during periods of rising FEDFUNDS, contradicting the resilience mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate margins and prompt anticipatory rate hikes, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of observations across a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.0 percent signals economic contraction that reduces earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 58 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and stagnant unemployment rates indicates labor hoarding during uncertainty, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence metric shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase confirms weak manufacturing demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines coincide with positive 5-day returns in over 65 percent of cases during recovery phases, invalidating the weakness signal.
